summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Gravitational PE (universal)'?

"Gravitational PE (universal)" means: U = -GMm/r; defined as zero at infinite separation; always negative for bound systems. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Total energy in orbit'?

"Total energy in orbit" means: E = KE + PE = ½mv² - GMm/r = -GMm/(2r) for circular orbits; negative means bound. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Escape condition'?

"Escape condition" means: E ≥ 0; when kinetic energy equals or exceeds |PE|, the object can escape to infinity. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Binding energy'?

"Binding energy" means: The energy required to completely separate a system; equals |E_total| for a gravitationally bound system. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Energy conservation in orbits'?

"Energy conservation in orbits" means: ½mv₁² - GMm/r₁ = ½mv₂² - GMm/r₂; used to find speed at any orbital distance without knowing forces. The other options describe different concepts.
